summary: Addresses external review #8 by adding symmetric API-server default normalization for Deployment, Service, CronJob, and Job specs. Three new files in internal/common/normalize/ (deployment.go, service.go, cronjob.go) fill in well-known Kubernetes API-server defaults before spec comparison, preventing infinite update loops and spurious reconcile updates. Callers in deployment.go and job.go now DeepCopy and normalize both desired and existing specs before DeepEqual comparison. Comprehensive unit tests verify default-filling and explicit-value preservation for all new functions.

tests_checklist.item: All unit tests pass (13 tests in normalize/)
Default-filling verified for all API-server defaults per resource type
Explicit-value preservation tested (zero values like BackoffLimit=0 preserved)
Edge case: Recreate strategy does not get RollingUpdate params
Delegation verified (CronJobSpecDefaults calls JobSpecDefaults and PodSpecDefaults)

code_quality_checklist.item: SPDX headers on all new files
Feature: CC-0005 tag in all test files
Functions are small, single-responsibility (<20 lines each)
No dead code or commented-out code
golangci-lint and go vet pass with 0 issues

security_checklist.item: No user input processed — pure normalization of API structs

architecture_checklist.item: Symmetric normalization pattern: both desired and existing specs normalized before comparison

dry_yagni_checklist.item: JobSpecDefaults extracted and reused by both CronJobSpecDefaults and RunJob (via PodSpecDefaults)
No over-engineering — only defaults that cause known comparison failures are included

fail_fast_checklist.item: Nil pointer guards before dereferencing in all normalize functions

defensive_checklist.item: DeepCopy used on both specs before mutation, preventing caller state corruption

suggested_improvements: Minor: IPFamilyPolicy (SingleStack) mentioned in external review was intentionally omitted from ServiceSpecDefaults — this is acceptable since IPFamilyPolicy behavior depends on cluster configuration, but could be revisited if spurious Service updates are observed in dual-stack clusters

detected_patterns.name: Symmetric DeepCopy-then-normalize before spec comparison
detected_patterns.applies_when: Comparing a desired Kubernetes resource spec against the existing server-returned spec to decide whether an update is needed
detected_patterns.description: Both the desired and existing specs are DeepCopied, then normalized via the appropriate normalize.*Defaults function, before being compared with apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual. This prevents both false positives (spurious updates from API-server-injected defaults) and ensures neither the caller's object nor the cached existing object is mutated.

detected_patterns.name: Hierarchical normalize.*Defaults with delegation
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding a new resource-type normalization function to the normalize/ package (e.g., StatefulSetSpecDefaults)
detected_patterns.description: Each resource-level normalize function fills in its own API-server defaults, then delegates to the next lower level. CronJobSpecDefaults calls JobSpecDefaults and PodSpecDefaults. DeploymentSpecDefaults calls PodSpecDefaults. This avoids duplication and ensures pod-level defaults are always applied regardless of which entry point is used. New resource normalizers must follow this delegation chain.

detected_patterns.name: Normalize function test pair: defaults + preservesExplicitValues
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding tests for a new normalize.*Defaults function
detected_patterns.description: Every normalize function has exactly two tests: TestXxxDefaults verifies that zero/nil fields are filled with correct API-server defaults (including delegation to lower-level normalizers), and TestXxxDefaults_preservesExplicitValues verifies that non-zero fields are left unchanged. Both use NewGomegaWithT(t) and gomega matchers.
